info.brief: Functions and constants for interacting with webview APIs
info.description: Functions and constants for interacting with webview APIs

elements.name: webview.create
elements.description:
Creates a webview instance. It can show HTML pages as well as evaluate Javascript. The view remains hidden until the first call. There can exist a maximum of 4 webviews at the same time.
On iOS, the callback will never get a `webview.CALLBACK_RESULT_EVAL_ERROR`, due to the iOS SDK implementation."

parameters.name: callback
parameters.doc: A callback which receives info about finished requests taking the following parameters:

elements.name: webview.destroy
elements.description: Destroys an instance of a webview.

parameters.name: webview_id
parameters.doc: The webview id (returned by the `webview.create()` call)

elements.name: webview.open_raw
elements.description: Opens a web page in the webview, using HTML data. Once the request is done, the callback (registered in `webview.create()`) is invoked.

parameters.name: options
parameters.doc: A table of options for the request. See `webview.open()`

elements.name: webview.eval
elements.description: Evaluates JavaScript within the context of the currently loaded page (if any). Once the request is done, the callback (registered in `webview.create()`) is invoked. The callback will get the result in the `data["result"]` field.

parameters.name: transparent
parameters.doc: If `true`, the webview background becomes transparent, otherwise opaque.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.doc: If `0`, hides the webview. If non zero, shows the view

elements.name: webview.is_visible
elements.description: Returns the visibility state of the webview.

elements.name: webview.set_position
elements.description: Sets the position and size of the webview

parameters.name: x
parameters.doc: The x position of the webview

parameters.name: y
parameters.doc: The y position of the webview

parameters.name: width
parameters.doc: The width of the webview (-1 to match screen width)

parameters.name: height
parameters.doc: The height of the webview (-1 to match screen height)
